hypothesis: If a reinforcement-learning generative model maximizes CATS-based pharmacophoric similarity to 53 estrogen receptor antagonists while penalizing MACCS/MAP4-based structural similarity, then it will generate novel, synthetically accessible, high-QED molecules with significantly better QVina docking scores against the estrogen receptor (PDB 8AWG) than a QED+docking baseline because pharmacophore-focused optimization preserves key interaction motifs while promoting scaffold diversity.
